# Betti Publish Guide

Parent: [Gnosis Polyglot](../README.md)

Publishing and economics for artifact authors: storing an artifact with your
DID and a price, getting it onto a deployed surface, how you earn on cache
replays (the 70/30 split), how callers are metered, and the admitted-cache-key
mint from the author's side.

Companions: [BETTI_WASM_GUIDE.md](./BETTI_WASM_GUIDE.md) (writing and
compiling), [BETTI_WASM_REFERENCE.md](./BETTI_WASM_REFERENCE.md) (the
manifest your artifact carries), [BETTI_MESH_GUIDE.md](./BETTI_MESH_GUIDE.md)
(token burn on scheduled work). The operator-side money manual — enablement
state, kill switches, reconciliation queries — is
[BETTI_ECONOMICS_OPERATIONS.md](../../../x-gnosis/docs/BETTI_ECONOMICS_OPERATIONS.md);
the authoritative mint design and runbook is
[BETTI_CACHE_MINT_DESIGN.md](../../../x-gnosis/docs/BETTI_CACHE_MINT_DESIGN.md).

## 1. Store an artifact: `gnode wasm-store`

`gnode wasm-store` shells the Betti emitter and lands the module + manifest
pair in a local file registry (`gnosis/src/betti-wasm-cli.ts`):

```console
$ pnpm run gnode -- wasm-store open-source/gnosis/gnode/benchmarks/echo.ts \
    --export app --out-dir ./my-registry \
    --owner-did did:key:zExampleAuthor --price-cents 2
{
  "artifactId": "32510684bcf0fef7",
  "key": "a0ncdfa04bf8023",
  "abi": "json-v1",
  "tier": "certified-direct",
  "byteLength": 563,
  "sha256": "b582e3b48b51aee8f67918ce2970091265d63bae298c28288c0023a8ef02de00",
  "wasmPath": ".../my-registry/betti/v1/32510684bcf0fef7.wasm",
  "manifestPath": ".../my-registry/betti/v1/32510684bcf0fef7.manifest.json",
  "registryPath": ".../my-registry/registry.json",
  "ownerDid": "did:key:zExampleAuthor",
  "priceCents": 2
}
```

(Output above is a verbatim run from 2026-07-03.) Layout: `registry.json`
(the index) plus `betti/v1/<artifactId>.wasm` and
`betti/v1/<artifactId>.manifest.json`.

Flags: `--export <fn>` (required), `--out-dir <dir>` (registry root),
`--emitter <path>` (pin the monster/gnexec binary; otherwise the standard
resolution order — set `GNOSIS_MONSTER_BIN` to override), and the two
ownership fields:

- `--owner-did <did>` — the DID that owns this artifact. This is the
  identity that gets paid (section 4).
- `--price-cents <n>` — the listing price per replayed execution.

Both are optional; an artifact stored without them is unowned/unpriced —
it still runs and callers are still metered, but replay earnings route to
the house rather than to you (section 5).

Persistence beyond the local directory is binding-injected: the same store
module (`gnosis/src/betti-wasm-store.ts`) writes a D1 table
(`betti_wasm_artifacts`, including `owner_did` and `price_cents` columns)
and R2 objects under `betti/v1/*` when a worker provides those bindings.
Loads re-verify `sha256` against the manifest — a tampered artifact refuses
to load. The production registry (D1 `betti-registry`,
`f2b15716-e7e9-45a8-8014-efefe79f8e95`, plus the `betti-registry` R2 bucket)
is live, integrity-verified on seed, and was seeded 2026-07-03 with the two
demo artifacts (`32510684bcf0fef7` echo, `e888b5ef029ad0eb` numeric-loop) —
both unowned/unpriced, so their replay evidence routes to the house lane.
Seeding into it is an operator action
(`gnosis/scripts/seed-betti-registry.ts`; see the
[economics operations doc](../../../x-gnosis/docs/BETTI_ECONOMICS_OPERATIONS.md)).

## 2. Get it serving: `gnode wasm-codegen` + deploy

Cloudflare Workers cannot compile wasm at runtime, so deployed surfaces
import artifacts at deploy time. `wasm-codegen` turns a registry directory
into that import map:

```console
$ pnpm run gnode -- wasm-codegen ./my-registry \
    --out open-source/x-gnosis/src/workers/generated/betti-artifacts.ts
```

It emits a `betti-artifacts.ts` module of `CompiledWasm` imports plus the
manifests (it does not invoke the emitter — codegen is registry-to-module
only). Then redeploy the artifact surface from its own directory with the
sovereign deploy verb:

```console
$ cd open-source/x-gnosis/workers/betti-wasm-demo && a0 xr deploy
```

Your artifact then appears in `GET /wasm/artifacts` and executes via
`POST /wasm/execute {artifactId, input}`. `swerve mesh-status` shows
desired-vs-live drift between a local registry and the deployed surface
([mesh guide, section 9](./BETTI_MESH_GUIDE.md#9-swerve-as-a-mesh-client-b4)).

## 3. How callers are charged (what your price does)

Metering wraps execution on metered surfaces
(`x-gnosis/src/betti-wasm-metering.ts` -> the edgework
`/api/internal/deduct-credits` route). Per execution:

- **Cold run** (artifact actually executed): the caller is charged the flat
  per-execution cost — 1 cent by default. This flat cost is a listing-price
  floor, not cost recovery. Your `price_cents` rides along as the
  reference cost when the registry knows the artifact.
- **Cache hit** (ESC replay of a pure artifact's result for a repeat
  `(artifactId, input)`): actual cost 0 — and the charging route bills the
  caller actual + 30% of the savings. Cheap replays are the caller's
  incentive to reuse; the savings share is the platform's.

Metering is deliberately fail-open: a metering outage can never break
compute. Only artifacts with `pure: true` in the manifest participate in
result caching at all. Mesh scheduling has its own admission-time meter
(token burn, [mesh guide section 8](./BETTI_MESH_GUIDE.md#8-token-burn));
that lane settles fail-open too.

## 4. How you earn: the 70/30 replay split

Owner earn (Wave D3) triggers on **cache hits only**, and only for artifacts
with both an `ownerDid` and `priceCents > 0`:

```text
per ESC hit:  credit_owner( floor(priceCents * 0.70) )
```

The remaining 30% is the platform's side of the replay. Cold runs do not
generate owner earn — the economics reward publishing artifacts whose
results get REUSED, not artifacts that merely execute. Practical
consequences for authors:

- `pure: true` is what makes you money: impure artifacts never cache, so
  they never replay, so they never pay.
- Price what a replay is worth: your earn per replay is
  `floor(priceCents * 0.7)` — at `--price-cents 2` each replay credits 1
  cent; below 2 cents `floor` rounds your share to 0.
- Your own replays of your own artifact are taxed by the same 30% — replay
  farming yourself is analyzed and defended in the design doc (section 5).

## 5. The cache mint, from the author's side (Wave D4)

Beyond per-replay earn, an artifact whose cache key accumulates real usage
evidence can be admitted for an EDGEWORK mint. Status: **enabled in
production since 2026-07-03** — `BETTI_CACHE_MINT_ENABLED="true"` on both
the evidence side (edgework) and the mint side (dashrelay,
`POST /api/provision/edgework-cache-mint`, which answers `401` to anything
unauthorized — verified live). Mints are operator-invoked, not automatic:
the flag being on means evidence is accruing and the route exists, not that
minting happens on a timer.

What counts as evidence, and the v1 admission thresholds (from the design
doc, section 2; env-tunable):

| Requirement | v1 value |
| --- | --- |
| ESC-hit ledger rows for the artifact within the qualification window | >= 100 (`BETTI_CACHE_MINT_MIN_HITS`) |
| Distinct paying `userId`s among those rows | >= 3 |
| Distinct payers excluding the owner | >= 2 (your own replays count at a discount, not in full) |
| Qualification window | 30 days |

Mint size: `min(hits * price_cents * 10%, $5)` per artifact per window
(`BETTI_CACHE_MINT_BPS = 1000`, `BETTI_CACHE_MINT_WINDOW_CAP_CENTS = 500`),
under a global rolling-24h cap of $50 across all cache mints. Windows renew:
an artifact can qualify again in a later window with renewed evidence;
idempotency keys (`betti-cache:<artifactId>:<windowEnd>`) prevent
double-minting the same window.

House policy for anonymous/unowned usage: when the artifact has no owner
DID (or the beneficiary cannot be resolved), the mint routes to the house
DID (`did:ambient:forkjoin:house`) rather than being dropped — recorded
with `beneficiary_kind: 'house'`. As an author this means: **set
`--owner-did` before your artifact becomes popular** — evidence accrued
while unowned mints to the house, not retroactively to you.

Abuse economics are engineered against self-replay farming and sybil payers
(distinct-payer thresholds, the 30% tax on owner-funded replays, per-window
and global caps) — read the design doc's section 5 before trying to be
clever; the caps make it not worth it by construction.

Current enablement state, kill switches, and the reconciliation queries all
live operator-side:
[BETTI_ECONOMICS_OPERATIONS.md](../../../x-gnosis/docs/BETTI_ECONOMICS_OPERATIONS.md)
(state table) and
[BETTI_CACHE_MINT_DESIGN.md section 8](../../../x-gnosis/docs/BETTI_CACHE_MINT_DESIGN.md)
(runbook).

## 6. Landing

- Consumer product wave (in development): `edgework deploy` CLI verb,
  `/api/v1/functions`, and the `fn.edgework.ai` gateway — a hosted
  publish-and-serve front end over this pipeline. Not usable yet; the
  `gnode wasm-store` + `wasm-codegen` + `a0 xr deploy` path above is the
  current real path.

---

Verified against: monorepo 2026-07-03 (root `2d9e62bc`, gnosis `9ed4e508`,
x-gnosis `337a6d3f`); the `wasm-store` run, the live mint-route `401` probe,
and the live demo-worker artifact listing were executed that day. Backing
test suites:
`gnosis/src/__tests__/{betti-wasm-store,betti-wasm-codegen,betti-wasm-execution}.test.ts`,
`x-gnosis/src/__tests__/betti-wasm-metering.test.ts`,
`apps/dashrelay-app/src/index.betti-cache-mint.test.ts`,
`apps/edgework-app/src/lib/internal-credit-authority.betti-evidence.test.ts`.
